# CASA00025 Group Project Title Here

## Project Summary 

This project develops an interactive Google Earth Engine application to assess glacier change and future retreat risk across Greenland’s peripheral glaciers. The application combines two linked components: a Landsat-based glacier extent comparison (2000–2024) using NDSI mapping, and a Random Forest model predicting retreat susceptibility using seven topographic, climatic, and human-environment predictors, including elevation, slope, temperature trends, and proximity measures. This dual framework enables users to compare observed glacier change with modelled future risk, translating cryosphere analysis into an accessible platform for environmental assessment and climate adaptation planning.

Fill in the sections below to provide a brief summary of your project. Each section should have no more than 100 words. Do not edit any of the headings.

### Problem Statement 

Greenland’s peripheral glaciers are undergoing rapid change under climatic warming, yet spatial tools linking observed retreat with predictive risk assessment remain limited. Existing studies often focus on monitoring glacier loss or modelling controls separately, making it difficult to explore both historical change and future vulnerability within one framework. This application addresses that gap by integrating glacier mapping, warming trends, and multi-factor machine learning risk prediction into an interactive platform. It supports understanding of where retreat has occurred, where future retreat is likely, and how environmental and anthropogenic factors may shape glacier vulnerability.

What is the problem you’re trying to address using this application? 

### End User 

The application is designed for users involved in cryosphere research, environmental planning, climate adaptation, and Arctic governance. These users require scientifically robust but interpretable tools to assess glacier change and identify vulnerable regions. By delivering remote sensing analysis and predictive modelling through an interactive web platform rather than static research outputs, the application improves accessibility for researchers, policymakers, and wider stakeholders. It supports evidence-based decision-making, environmental monitoring, and communication of glacier risk in the context of rapid Arctic change.

Who are you building this application for? How does it address a need this community has?

### Data

What data are you using? 

### Methodology

How are you using this data to address the problem?

### Interface

How does your application's interface work to address the needs of your end user?

## The Application 

Replace the link below with the link to your application.

:::{.column-page}

<iframe src='https://ollielballinger.users.earthengine.app/view/turkey-earthquake' width='100%' height='700px'></iframe>

:::
## How it Works 

Use this section to explain how your application works using code blocks and text explanations (no more than 500 words excluding code):

```js
Map.setCenter(35.51898, 33.90153, 15);

Map.setOptions("satellite");

var aoi = ee.Geometry.Point(35.51898, 33.90153).buffer(3000);
```

You can include images:

![Pixelwise T-Test, 2018](images/beirut_change_2020.jpg)

and math:
$$ \Large t = {\frac{\overline{x_1}-\overline{x_2}} {\sqrt{\frac{s^2_1}{n_1} + \frac{s^2_2}{n_2}}}} $$
